mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It allows platform operators to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4

```

> This command adds the repository for the latest version.

steps.title: Install Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are essential for Cluster API to function as they define the custom resources used by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace, allowing you to manage clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

```

> This command installs the Cluster API components.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Create a Cluster API Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource that defines the desired state of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API components running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster state. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Check the status of your nodes before upgrading.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that the upgraded components are running.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This suggests that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct infrastructure provider CRDs and components are installed.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if there are misconfigurations in the Cluster API components.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system and fix any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network configuration and ensure that the API server is running and accessible.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version for Cluster resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your Cluster resources to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your created clusters and their statuses.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified tools installed.
